author: Niche Gamer
text:
Waifu Wednesday: Evie from Stellar Blade BLOOD RAIN Welcome back to Waifu Wednesday, Niche Gamer’s column where we talk about characters, their appeal, and a little on their history. This week’s waifu is Evie from the upcoming game Stellar Blade: BLOOD RAIN. Stellar Blade: BLOOD RAIN is the upcoming sequel to 2024’s Stellar Blade from developer SHIFT UP. At the time, Stellar Blade made waves […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Here are the biggest Steam wishlist winners from Summer Game Fest 2026 Summer Game Fest 2026 may be over, but its impact is already showing up where publishers care most: Steam wishlists. According to new estimates shared by industry analyst Rhys Elliott of Aliena Analytics, Resident Evil Veronica emerged as the event’s biggest winner between June 2 and June 8, adding an impressive 522,000 new wishlists.  Not […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Stupid Never Dies reveals fall release and new trailer GPTRACK50 has shared a new trailer for Stupid Never Dies alongside confirming a fall release for the game. Stupid Never Dies is now set to launch sometime this fall for Windows PC (Steam) and PlayStation 5. Here’s a rundown on the new details, plus the new trailer: About Stupid Never Dies Set in a monster-ruled otherworldly […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Valve phasing out physical Steam gift cards to combat scammers Valve has confirmed that it will gradually phase out physical Steam Gift Cards sold in stores, citing the continued abuse of the cards by fraudsters targeting vulnerable victims. While physical cards won’t disappear overnight, the company says it will stop producing new retail stock and instead focus on digital alternatives available directly through Steam. Gift […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Rhythm Heaven Groove gets new overview trailer Publisher and developer Nintendo has shared a new gameplay overview trailer for Rhythm Heaven Groove, their long-awaited music-rhythm action game. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus the new trailer: Tune in and groove Get ready to use your ears! Time your button presses to the beat across a splashy collection of games that will […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Nintendo shares drop after Direct leaves investors wanting more Nintendo’s latest Direct presentation delivered plenty of announcements, but it wasn’t enough to impress investors. Following the roughly 50-minute showcase on June 9, Nintendo’s share price reportedly fell between 7% and 10%, marking one of the company’s sharpest declines in recent weeks. While fans were treated to a long list of upcoming games for both […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
L’Empereur gets modern re-release Publisher Hamster Corporation has announced the next Console Archives release is L’Empereur, the classic 80s simulation game set in Napoleonic France. L’Empereur is launching this week on June 11th for both Switch 2 and PlayStation 5, worldwide. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus a new trailer: Only you can lead the French to victory over Europe! […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Rack ‘Em Up gets modern re-release Hamster Corporation has announced the latest Arcade Archives re-release will be Rack ‘Em Up, the classic 80s billiards game by Konami. Rack ‘Em Up is getting released via Arcade Archives 2 for Xbox Series X|S, Switch 2, and PS5 and Arcade Archives for both Switch and PS4. The game is launching on June 11th, pending your local […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Unrailed 2: Back on Track preview Unrailed 2: Back on Track is a frantic multiplayer-forward title releasing soon on home consoles, where you and your friends must hurry to lay tracks as a locomotive is heading down the tracks.

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Bradley the Badger gets new publisher Focus Entertainment Focus Entertainment has officially partnered with Day 4 Night Studios to publish Bradley the Badger, an upcoming action-adventure platformer from a team led by industry veterans Christian Cantamessa, known for his work on Red Dead Redemption, and Davide Soliani, the creative mind behind the Mario + Rabbids series. The game, which was revealed at The […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
1666: Amsterdam Preview Having just recently been revealed during Summer Game Fest, 1666: Amsterdam has already received a playable prologue on Steam. Usually, when developers release a prologue, it’s meant to serve as a small slice of the game, something that introduces the story, showcases the core gameplay loop, and leaves players wanting more. After spending around 30 […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
XBOX gives rare update on The Elder Scrolls VI after years of silence Speaking in a recent interview, XBOX Chief Content Officer Matt Booty revealed that he recently visited Bethesda Game Studios, where he met with Todd Howard and saw The Elder Scrolls VI running internally. Booty said (via Variety) the game is “coming along well” and looks “amazing,” offering a rare glimpse behind the curtain for one of […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Beautiful anime life sim game Vivarium launches in 2027 Revealed during the Xbox Games Showcase 2026, we finally got another look at Vivarium, the latest project from Studio Meadowflower and publisher Serenity Forge, developed in collaboration with Crunchyroll and Shochiku Games.  You take on the role of 11-year-old Jenny, a cheerful resident of a tiny town contained entirely within a terrarium. Life appears simple […]
